race.name: Schleck Gran Fondo
race.tagline: 159km of Luxembourg Ardennes brutality on Fränk Schleck's savage training roads—UCI qualifier that sells out faster than a Tour de France sprint.

vitals.field_size: Sells out fast; hundreds in Gran Fondo waves, open to all ages over 18 with elite qualifiers pushing 40km/h averages.
vitals.registration: Early bird pasta party access, race packet surprises, timing chip, medals, jersey option, bike rental; costs unlisted but value-packed for UCI prestige.
vitals.prize_purse: No cash; UCI World Series qualification spots, category podiums, team awards by aggregate times.
vitals.aid_stations: Standard Gran Fondo support on open roads with police escorts at intersections.
vitals.cutoff_time: Flexible for leisure riders; competitive waves target sub-5 hours for qualifiers.
distance_options: Gran Fondo: 98.7mi, 7874ft — UCI qualifier beast through Ardennes and Moselle with steep punches
Medio Fondo: ~56mi, ~4593ft — Women's/all-ages qualifier with shortcut climbs
Leisure: 98.7mi, 7874ft — Later start for chill riders on full course
Team: Gran/Medio aggregates of 5 fastest times

climate.description: Luxembourg May delivers 50-68°F temps ideal for hard efforts, but pack arm warmers for morning chills and rain that turns Ardennes roads slick. Perfect weather fueled past epics, though cramps lurk if hydration slips. Annual variance demands flexibility.

terrain.surface: Smooth Luxembourg asphalt winds through Moselle vineyards and Mullerthal forests; open to traffic but policed at crossings. Character screams classic Euro gran fondo: relentless punchy climbs over 11%, fast descents, no long HC monsters but cumulative suffering.

gravel_god_rating.tier_note: UCI qualifier prestige catapults it to T3 solidity, but low altitude and neutralised starts temper raw adventure. Sells out on Schleck name alone—solid Euro mass-participation hitter without Tour-level hype. Lacks gravel-god remoteness but nails accessible suffering.

logistics.explanation: Mondorf's compact start/finish shuns airport hassles—LUX is 25km away, hotels steps from line. Neutralised rollouts smartly dodge chaos, police keep open roads sane. Bike park and showers seal pro-level polish, though traffic demands vigilance.

length.explanation: 159km hits gran fondo sweet spot—long enough to shatter weekend warriors, short enough for sub-5 reality. Mirrors Schleck training brutality without Giro Rosa excess. Qualifier distance demands respect, no half-measures here.

technicality.explanation: Punchy 11% ramps and tech descents reward bike handlers, but neutralisation dulls early fireworks. Open roads add real-world edge over closed-circuit snoozes. Solid, not savage—perfect for age-groupers dodging pro peloton stress.

elevation.explanation: 2400m cumulative grind via short-steep hells mimics Ardennes classics—pure leg-burner sans altitude hypoxia. Mullerthal punches accumulate like interest on a bad loan. Elite qualifiers average 40km/h despite it; mortals suffer gloriously.

climate.explanation: May spring goldilocks: crisp starts, warming climbs, rare heat cramps. Rain jazzes descents without Strade Bianche mud hell. Luxembourg luck beats Alpine snowouts—train wet, race dry, profit.

altitude.explanation: Sea-level snoozer—no thin air drama, just pure wattage wars. Fine for qualifiers, boring for hypoxia chasers. Keeps it accessible; pros won't bonk, amateurs won't puke.

adventure.explanation: Schleck's Moselle vineyards and forests deliver postcard pain—feels like sneaking pro routes. Team relays and leisure options amp inclusivity. Open-road frisson beats tape-sealed tedium.

prestige.explanation: UCI World Series golden ticket—Schleck brothers' stamp screams legitimacy. Sells out on name alone; qualifiers hit 41km/h insanity. Euro gran fondo royalty, no question.

race_quality.explanation: Neutralised starts, chip timing, pasta parties with Fränk—pro touches abound. High field depth crushes casuals early. Podium vibes and medals ship worldwide; execution nails it.

experience.explanation: Pasta fete with Schlecks, post-race bash, secure park—feels like family reunion with suffering. Kids' events and teams broaden appeal. Unforgettable Euro gran fondo theatre.

community.explanation: Luxembourg cycling passion pulses—local club co-org adds authenticity. Age-group warriors and leisure rollers mingle seamlessly. Schleck halo binds it tight.

field_depth.explanation: Qualifier speeds hit 41km/h—deep talent pool shreds stragglers. Waves manage chaos, but front packs are pro-adjacent feral. No sandbaggers survive.

value.explanation: UCI qual for presumed reasonable euro delivers outsized ROI. Pasta, jersey, timing—loaded packet. Prestige punches above weight class.

expenses.explanation: Euro travel bites, but local lodging gems like 450m hotels slash logistics. Flights to LUX cheapish; entry fair. Not budget-basement, but prestige justifies.

biased_opinion.summary: Fränk Schleck bottled his Tour-crushing roads into a sellout UCI qualifier that humbles amateurs while wowing with Moselle magic. Punchy climbs and 40km/h elites make it brutally democratic—join the fast or get dropped. Pasta parties with the legend himself elevate beyond mere miles. Neutral starts smarten safety, open roads thrill. T3 gem for qualifiers chasing worlds.
biased_opinion.bottom_line: Pin this if UCI qual dreams burn; elite depth will punish pretenders. Euro classic worth the flight—suffer like Schleck, medal like a champ.

final_verdict.one_liner: Schleck's Ardennes punchfest: UCI glory for the gritty.
final_verdict.should_you_race: Yes if you're qualifier-hunting or crave Schleck routes—train punchies. Skip if hating open roads or neutral snoozes; pure climbers yawn.
final_verdict.alternatives: Gran Fondo New York (gfny) for urban prestige, Marmotte (marmotte) for Alpine elevation, Nove Colli (nove-colli) for Italian rollers.

logistics.airport: Luxembourg Findel (LUX) 25km north—25min drive/taxi to Mondorf. Budget carriers galore; rent van for teams.
logistics.lodging_strategy: Book Mondorf gems 450m from line for zero-hassle; Legendre 20km backup affordable. Airbnbs vanish fast—secure early.
logistics.food: Pre-race pasta bash free for early regs; Moselle vineyards post-ride. Local gems fuel without fuss.
logistics.packet_pickup: Race morning at Mondorf HQ—surprises, chip, jersey swap seamless.
logistics.parking: Secure bike park onsite; ample lots near start/finish.

history.founder: Fränk Schleck, post-pro icon via Schleck X-Perience SA with ACC Contern
history.origin_story: Post-2016 retirement, Fränk Schleck channeled 13 pro years into sharing Luxembourg's Moselle and Ardennes gems. First 2017 edition exploded as UCI qualifier on his exact training roads. Brothers Andy and Fränk host, blending pro polish with amateur fire—now annual sellout ritual.
history.reputation: Schleck seal guarantees quality—high-speed qualifiers and family vibes. International draw for worlds spots; locals beam with pride.

course_description.character: Relentless Ardennes-Moselle rollercoaster: short-steep climbs explode groups early, river flats scream at 40km/h, forests hide punchy walls. Asphalt perfection through vineyards and 'Little Switzerland'—visual feast masking leg apocalypse. Neutral 9.5km rollout builds tension before 11% wake-up.

suffering_zones.label: Neutral End Wall
suffering_zones.desc: 9.5km neutralisation dumps into 2km @11% ramp—first selection shatters waves. Descend to river, then flats tear echelons. Cramps loom if hydration lags.
suffering_zones.terrain_detail: Steep asphalt pitch to fast river road

suffering_zones.label: Mullerthal Punches
suffering_zones.desc: Mid-race kicks through 'Little Switzerland'—repeated 9-11%ers accumulate 2400m toll. Groups splinter, solos suffer isolation. Vistas mock the pain.

suffering_zones.label: Moselle Grind
suffering_zones.desc: Vineyard climbs drag late-race fatigue; short but savage, forcing survival mode. Final flats rally for Mondorf sprint. Elites average 33km/h here.

course_description.signature_challenge: Cumulative 2400m via endless 11% micro-ascents—no HC kings, just death by thousand cuts. Mirrors Schleck pro suffering; qualifiers demand 36km/h+ sustained.

guide_variables.race_terrain: Punchy road climbs through Ardennes and Moselle

non_negotiables.why: 11% walls post-neutral select brutally—untrained legs cramp by mile 50. Mimic course reps build threshold for 36km/h qualifiers. Skip, get isolated and dropped.
159km grind demands endurance; flats hit 40km/h in drafts. Test fueling to dodge 2/3-race fade. Essential for worlds qual contention.

citations.title: Schleck Gran Fondo: Race Report & Course Review

youtube_data.search_query: Schleck Gran Fondo gran fondo cycling Mondorf-les-Bains

videos.title: UCI - GF Schleck - Mondorf Les Bains
videos.description:
Came to the GF Schleck without much hopes, it was only one day ago I felt ok after being hit by a viral infection a week ago.
But as often you get plenty when you expect nothing.
This was my 5th edition and I got my best result ever. Ended up 25th out of 208 on a slightly tougher course as ussualy (159km - 2200Hm)
But then again you think "what if" I wasn't sick a week before ... maybe then ?!? :)
I think that there was an opportunity for a top 10, I climbed well for a non climber, but I felt a bit less powerfull then normally, certainly the 2nd part of the race.
But happy nevertheless !

Qualified for the Worlds in Australia ... amen
tags: tsbikes uci race gf granfondo road racing bike bikes

videos.title: Gran Fondo Schleck 2022 - 4K (my first fondo)
videos.description:
Some 4K footage from behind the saddle. 155km in 3h57m14s. ~2024hm, averaging 280W en NP 347W. Finished just outside top10 in M40-44 category, 5 seconds behind nr3, but was unable (and afraid) to position myself to the front in the final kilometers in the bunch I was in (around 100). I just did not want to crash in the last kilometers, after getting through the first 150km without trouble (but seeing several crashes/collisions).
0:00 Start: KM 0.0 KM 24.9
36:56 KM 75.3 to KM 78.7
44:38 KM 105.2 to KM 138.9
1:35:18 KM 140.9 to KM 141.3 (battery empty)
videos.curation_reason: First-person race recap with detailed performance metrics and course experience over 155km.
